response.reasoning: Step 1 — Experience length: the résumé spans roughly 2006–2026, about 19 years of professional software development, satisfying the 15-year minimum. Step 2 — Education: holds a BS in Computer Science, meeting the minimum; no Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, so the preferred academic bar is not met. Step 3 — Seniority trajectory: titles are predominantly Senior/Staff Software Developer at product companies; there is no evidence of director-level scope, organization-wide technical leadership, or strategic advisory work with executives or customers, which is central to this role. Step 4 — AI expertise: recent work at re:cinq includes AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow tooling — relevant but early and hands-on rather than 'recognized expertise' with demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale. Step 5 — Entrepreneurial signal: founded and sustained GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map, which maps well to the preferred entrepreneurial trait. Step 6 — Cloud/builder fluency: AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker, high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby, and polyglot depth (TypeScript, D, C/C++) show strong builder and design fluency. Step 7 — Overall: a strong, versatile senior/staff engineer who meets the minimums, but the gap to a director-level AI-strategy principal role — public thought leadership, strategic customer advisory, recognized AI track record — is substantial.
strengths: 19 years of hands-on engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Founder of GISCollective, showing sustained entrepreneurial initiative and platform-building
Recent practical AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling) plus cloud fluency (AWS CDK, Kubernetes)
concerns: No director/principal-level scope or evidence of driving org-wide or customer-facing technical strategy
AI experience is recent and tooling-focused, short of 'recognized expertise' with demonstrated AI products at scale
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and limited public thought leadership on AI/cloud strategy

key_factors.factor: Seniority gap vs director-level IC role
Entrepreneurial and broad builder track record

response.justification: The candidate comfortably meets the minimum qualifications with ~19 years of diverse engineering experience and brings genuine entrepreneurial energy through GISCollective, plus recent, relevant AI-agent infrastructure work. However, this is a director-level AI-strategy position requiring recognized AI expertise, strategic customer advisory, and organization-level influence — areas where the résumé shows little evidence, as the career has been primarily senior/staff hands-on delivery. The profile fits a strong staff engineer role better than a principal AI-strategy role, so an interview is only worthwhile if the panel wants to probe the recent AI work for hidden depth.
